summary: A low two-story rectangular compound with a large curved (semi-elliptical) red-brick tiled roof segment occupying most of the central volume, surrounded by a low white block perimeter and two tall square towers at opposite corners with overhanging layered caps. The main walls are pale (sandstone-like) with white vertical and horizontal corner/trim pillars. Multiple small horizontal windows run along both visible floors.

shape.overall_form: rectangular base with an attached central curved/rounded roof mass; two separate tall towers at corners connected by low walls/walkways
shape.symmetry: rough bilateral symmetry along the long axis (mirrored left/right elements) but with asymmetric roof curve and tower placement

materials.name: quartz
materials.role: corner/trim pillars and low perimeter blocks

materials.name: stonebrick
materials.role: tower cap edging and some trim

elements: continuous low white perimeter posts (~1 block high) enclosing a dirt/ground courtyard
two tall square towers (approx 3x3 or 4x4 columns) with multi-layered overhanging caps
main two-storey rectangular building with repeating small horizontal windows on both floors
large curved red-brick roof mass that is stepped (staircase/brick stairs) forming a semi-elliptical dome
raised central walkway/entrance projection with white pillars connecting to a small square forecourt
rebuild_hints: Start with a rectangular footprint (~29x47) surrounded by a 1-block-high white perimeter of spaced blocks; interior ground is dirt/brown.
Build the main two-floor shell from pale sandstone-like blocks with white quartz vertical corner pillars and a continuous white horizontal band between floors.
Place rows of small 1x1 glass windows spaced evenly across both floors; add an entrance projection centered on the long side with white pillars.
Construct the curved roof by layering red brick blocks and brick stairs to form a stepped semi-elliptical dome that covers the central mass and tapers to a single beacon/sea-lantern feature at top.
Add two tall towers at opposite corners (approx 12 blocks high) with light-colored faces, stonebrick/quartz corner accents and layered overhang caps topped with a sea-lantern or light block.
uncertainties: Exact block types: sandstone and quartz are inferred from color but may be alternative pale blocks (e.g., smooth sandstone, end stone, or diorite).
Precise internal layout and floor heights are not visible; floor estimate of 2 is based on external window bands.
Exact footprint dimensions are approximate from pixel/visual estimation and may be off by several blocks.
Tower cross-section (3x3 vs 4x4) and cap detailing are inferred from perspective; exact sizes may differ.
Ground surface inside perimeter appears brown/dirt but could be coarse dirt or terracotta.
